**Michael Julian Johnson**[[4]](#cite_note-4) (born June 4, 1986) is an American professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist"). Johnson currently fights in the [Lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight_(MMA) "Lightweight (MMA)") division of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC). A professional since 2008, Johnson mostly competed in his regional circuit, before signing onto the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") to appear on *[The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck")*, in which he was the runner-up.[[5]](#cite_note-tuf12-5)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Johnson was born in [St. Louis, Missouri](/wiki/St._Louis "St. Louis"), on June 4, 1986. He was raised as the youngest of three siblings. Johnson began fighting at the age of 10, after his father's fatal [heart attack](/wiki/Heart_attack "Heart attack"). The incident made Johnson want to exert his anger physically.[[6]](#cite_note-interview-6) Johnson later said, "I lost something, a huge part of me....Fighting was my way of not dealing with my dad's passing. Now that I think of it, of course, it was the wrong thing to do, especially putting all that burden on my mom having to deal with it."[[6]](#cite_note-interview-6) Athletic, Johnson was a three-sport varsity athlete at [Marquette High School](/wiki/Marquette_High_School_(Chesterfield,_Missouri) "Marquette High School (Chesterfield, Missouri)") in [Chesterfield, Missouri](/wiki/Chesterfield,_Missouri "Chesterfield, Missouri"). Johnson later received a full scholarship to play football at [Central Methodist University](/wiki/Central_Methodist_University "Central Methodist University"), but transferred after one year to [Meramec Community College](/wiki/Meramec_Community_College "Meramec Community College"), where he was one of the top NJCAA wrestlers in the country.[[3]](#cite_note-michaeljohnsonmma.com-3)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Johnson is a former Midwest Fight League and Xtreme Cage Fighting Champion who won those titles in July 2008 and October 2009, respectively.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Johnson trained at Springfield Fight Club, which is an affiliate of [Gracie Barra](/wiki/Gracie_Barra "Gracie Barra") based in [Springfield, Missouri](/wiki/Springfield,_Missouri "Springfield, Missouri"). Johnson also worked there as the assistant mixed martial arts coach.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

### *The Ultimate Fighter*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

Johnson then signed onto the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") circuit to appear on *[The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck")*.[[5]](#cite_note-tuf12-5)

Johnson's entry into the show was, in part, due to his persistence in attending tryouts for the show. After making the final interview round of *[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Nogueira vs. Team Mir](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Nogueira_vs._Team_Mir "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Nogueira vs. Team Mir")*, Johnson was told that he wasn't the right fit for the show.[[6]](#cite_note-interview-6) Johnson then tried out for the ninth season of the show, before finally becoming accepted onto the twelfth season after telling the producers, "I've been getting tired of chasing you [expletive] around the country. I'm here; I'm not going to go anywhere. If you guys don't pick me, I'm going to try out again." Finally, Johnson was signed.[[6]](#cite_note-interview-6)

Johnson's first fight took place on the debut episode of the season, where he faced unbeaten [Pablo Garza](/wiki/Pablo_Garza_(fighter) "Pablo Garza (fighter)"). Johnson scored a unanimous decision victory, which moved him into the house.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In the second episode, both St-Pierre and Koscheck had Johnson at the top of their lists when it came time to select fighters. During the team selection process, however, St-Pierre created a fake list that did not have Johnson among his top selections and subtly showed it to Koscheck to fool him into believing it. This seemed to manipulate Koscheck into picking Marc Stevens, as he may have believed he could take Johnson later. This allowed St-Pierre to choose Johnson with the second overall pick.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Johnson competed in his preliminary round matchup against Aaron Wilkinson in the third episode. After some early striking exchanges in the first round, Wilkinson was able to take Johnson down and utilize ground-and-pound punches and elbows. In the second round, Johnson landed an early superman punch, as well as a takedown. After takedowns were achieved by both men, Johnson stuffed a final attempt and took the second round on the judges' scorecards. In the sudden victory round, Johnson attacked quickly, which allowed him to end the fight via a rear-naked choke.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In episode 8, Team GSP had to pick which team members would fight each other (considering they had 5 members in the quarter-finals). St. Pierre asked each member to pick the fighter they wanted to fight. Johnson and [Alex Caceres](/wiki/Alex_Caceres "Alex Caceres") picked each other and a fight was scheduled.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Johnson won by unanimous decision after two rounds, earning himself a spot in the semi-finals.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In the final round, Johnson faced the one remaining Team Koscheck member, [Nam Phan](/wiki/Nam_Phan "Nam Phan"). In a back-and-forth fight, Johnson scored takedowns. The consensus after the fight was that Johnson won the opening round, and Phan the second. The third was in dispute, however, with each fighter's coach convinced they had won the round. Johnson was declared the winner via split decision.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

#### 2010

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2010")]

Johnson made his UFC debut in [The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_GSP_vs._Team_Koscheck_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team GSP vs. Team Koscheck Finale") against [Jonathan Brookins](/wiki/Jonathan_Brookins "Jonathan Brookins"), on December 14, 2010. That fight determined the winner of The Ultimate Fighter 12. After an impressive first round, Johnson lost rounds two and three — and subsequently the fight — via unanimous decision, leaving him the runner-up.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

#### 2011

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2011")]

Johnson was looking to take a fight outside of the UFC before returning to the promotion. The fight was to headline the North American Fighting Championship card against Jim Bleau.[[17]](#cite_note-17) However, after obtaining a UFC fight, Johnson pulled out of the NAFC bout to focus his training on the UFC bout. Johnson faced [Edward Faaloloto](/wiki/Edward_Faaloloto "Edward Faaloloto") on June 26, 2011, at [UFC on Versus 4](/wiki/UFC_on_Versus_4 "UFC on Versus 4")[[18]](#cite_note-18) and won the fight via TKO in the first round.

Johnson faced [Paul Sass](/wiki/Paul_Sass "Paul Sass") on October 1, 2011, at [UFC on Versus 6](/wiki/UFC_on_Versus_6 "UFC on Versus 6").[[19]](#cite_note-19) He lost the fight via submission in the first round.

#### 2012

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2012")]

Johnson was expected to face [Cody McKenzie](/wiki/Cody_McKenzie "Cody McKenzie") on January 28, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 2](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_2 "UFC on Fox 2"). However, McKenzie was forced out of the bout, with an injury, and he was replaced by [Shane Roller](/wiki/Shane_Roller "Shane Roller").[[20]](#cite_note-20) Johnson defeated Roller by unanimous decision, with all three judges scoring the bout 29–28.

Johnson faced [Tony Ferguson](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson "Tony Ferguson") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3"), replacing an injured [Thiago Tavares](/wiki/Thiago_Tavares "Thiago Tavares").[[21]](#cite_note-21) Johnson won the fight via unanimous decision.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Johnson was expected to face [Danny Castillo](/wiki/Danny_Castillo "Danny Castillo") on September 1, 2012, at [UFC 151](/wiki/UFC_151 "UFC 151").[[23]](#cite_note-23) However, after UFC 151 was cancelled, Johnson/Castillo was rescheduled and took place on October 5, 2012, at [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5").[[24]](#cite_note-24) After almost being finished in the first round, Johnson survived and came back to win via KO at 1:06 into the second round. Johnson was awarded *Knockout of the Night* honors for his performance.[[25]](#cite_note-UFCFX5-25)

Johnson fought [Myles Jury](/wiki/Myles_Jury "Myles Jury") on December 29, 2012, at [UFC 155](/wiki/UFC_155 "UFC 155").[[26]](#cite_note-26) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.

#### 2013

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2013")]

Johnson faced [Reza Madadi](/wiki/Reza_Madadi "Reza Madadi") on April 6, 2013, at [UFC on Fuel TV 9](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV_9 "UFC on Fuel TV 9").[[27]](#cite_note-27) Despite almost finishing Madadi with a head kick in the first round, Johnson eventually succumbed to the wrestling of Madadi and lost the fight via submission in the third round.

Johnson faced [Joe Lauzon](/wiki/Joe_Lauzon "Joe Lauzon") on August 17, 2013, at [UFC Fight Night 26](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_26 "UFC Fight Night 26").[[28]](#cite_note-28) He won the fight via unanimous decision, after outstriking Lauzon for all three rounds.

Johnson faced [Gleison Tibau](/wiki/Gleison_Tibau "Gleison Tibau") on December 28, 2013, at [UFC 168](/wiki/UFC_168 "UFC 168").[[29]](#cite_note-29) He won the fight by knockout in the second round.

#### 2014

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2014")]

Due to an injury to [Ross Pearson](/wiki/Ross_Pearson "Ross Pearson"), Johnson stepped in to face [Melvin Guillard](/wiki/Melvin_Guillard "Melvin Guillard") at [UFC Fight Night 37](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_37 "UFC Fight Night 37").[[30]](#cite_note-30) Johnson won the fight via unanimous decision.

Johnson was expected to face [Josh Thomson](/wiki/Josh_Thomson "Josh Thomson") on July 26, 2014, at [UFC on Fox 12](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_12 "UFC on Fox 12").[[31]](#cite_note-31)[[32]](#cite_note-32) However, on July 11, the UFC announced, that Johnson had been pulled from the bout.[[33]](#cite_note-33) It was later revealed that Johnson had been pulled from the bout and would not compete for the remainder of 2014 following his arrest in [Palm Beach County, Florida](/wiki/Palm_Beach_County,_Florida "Palm Beach County, Florida"), in April of that year.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

#### 2015

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: 2015")]

Johnson faced [Edson Barboza](/wiki/Edson_Barboza "Edson Barboza") on February 22, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 61](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_61 "UFC Fight Night 61").[[35]](#cite_note-35) He won the bout by unanimous decision.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

A bout with [Benson Henderson](/wiki/Benson_Henderson "Benson Henderson") was initially linked as the event headliner for [The Ultimate Fighter 21 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_American_Top_Team_vs._Blackzilians#The_Ultimate_Fighter_21_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: American Top Team vs. Blackzilians") on July 12, 2015.[[37]](#cite_note-37) Although never officially announced by the UFC, the bout between Henderson and Johnson will not take place at this event.[[38]](#cite_note-38)

Johnson faced [Beneil Dariush](/wiki/Beneil_Dariush "Beneil Dariush") on August 8, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 73](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_73 "UFC Fight Night 73").[[39]](#cite_note-39) Johnson lost the fight via controversial split decision.[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42) Every mainstream MMA media outlet polled by MMA Decisions scored the fight as decision victory for Johnson.[[43]](#cite_note-43)

Johnson faced [Nate Diaz](/wiki/Nate_Diaz "Nate Diaz") on December 19, 2015, at [UFC on Fox 17](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_17 "UFC on Fox 17").[[44]](#cite_note-44) He lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[45]](#cite_note-45) Both participants were awarded *Fight of the Night* honors.[[46]](#cite_note-UFCFox17-46)

#### 2016

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: 2016")]

A rematch with [Tony Ferguson](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson "Tony Ferguson") was expected to take place on March 5, 2016, at [UFC 196](/wiki/UFC_196 "UFC 196").[[47]](#cite_note-47) However, on January 27, it was announced, that Johnson withdrew from the bout, due to an injury.[[48]](#cite_note-48)

Johnson faced [Dustin Poirier](/wiki/Dustin_Poirier "Dustin Poirier") on September 17, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 94](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_94 "UFC Fight Night 94").[[49]](#cite_note-49) Johnson won the fight via knockout early in the first round with a sharp two-punch combination followed by ground and pound.[[50]](#cite_note-50) This win earned Johnson his first *Performance of the Night* bonus award.[[51]](#cite_note-UFCFN94-51)

Johnson next faced [Khabib Nurmagomedov](/wiki/Khabib_Nurmagomedov "Khabib Nurmagomedov") on November 12, 2016, at [UFC 205](/wiki/UFC_205 "UFC 205").[[52]](#cite_note-52) He lost the fight via submission in the third round.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

#### 2017

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: 2017")]

Johnson faced former [World Series of Fighting](/wiki/World_Series_of_Fighting "World Series of Fighting") champion [Justin Gaethje](/wiki/Justin_Gaethje "Justin Gaethje") on July 7, 2017, in the main event at [The Ultimate Fighter 25 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Redemption#The_Ultimate_Fighter_25_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Redemption").[[54]](#cite_note-54) Rocking Gaethje twice with punches, Johnson was not able to capitalize and lost the fight via TKO, with a combination of punches and knees in the second round.[[55]](#cite_note-55) The fight earned Johnson his second *Fight of the Night* bonus award.[[56]](#cite_note-UFCTUF25-56)

#### 2018

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: 2018")]

Johnson faced [Darren Elkins](/wiki/Darren_Elkins "Darren Elkins") in a featherweight bout on January 14, 2018, at [UFC Fight Night: Stephens vs. Choi](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_Stephens_vs._Choi "UFC Fight Night: Stephens vs. Choi").[[57]](#cite_note-57) He lost the fight via submission in the second round.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

Johnson faced [Andre Fili](/wiki/Andre_Fili "Andre Fili") on August 25, 2018, at [UFC Fight Night 135](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_135 "UFC Fight Night 135").[[59]](#cite_note-59) He won the fight via split decision.[[60]](#cite_note-60)

Johnson faced [Artem Lobov](/wiki/Artem_Lobov "Artem Lobov") on October 27, 2018, at [UFC Fight Night 138](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_138 "UFC Fight Night 138").[[61]](#cite_note-61) At weigh-ins, Johnson weighed in at 147 pounds, one pounds over the featherweight limit of 146 pounds. As a result, the fight proceeded at a catchweight bout and Johnson was to forfeit 20% of his purse to Lobov, however, Lobov refused to take the money.[[62]](#cite_note-62) Johnson defeated Lobov by unanimous decision.[[63]](#cite_note-63)

#### Consecutive losses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Consecutive losses")]

Johnson faced [Josh Emmett](/wiki/Josh_Emmett "Josh Emmett") on March 30, 2019, at [UFC on ESPN 2](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_2 "UFC on ESPN 2").[[64]](#cite_note-64) Despite being up on the judges scorecards, Johnson lost the fight via knockout, late in the third round.[[65]](#cite_note-65)[[66]](#cite_note-66)

Johnson faced [Stevie Ray](/wiki/Stevie_Ray_(fighter) "Stevie Ray (fighter)") at [UFC on ESPN+ 20](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN%2B_20 "UFC on ESPN+ 20") on October 26, 2019.[[67]](#cite_note-67) He lost the fight via majority decision.[[68]](#cite_note-68)

Johnson was scheduled to face [Evan Dunham](/wiki/Evan_Dunham "Evan Dunham") on [April 25, 2020](/wiki/Cancelled_UFC_event_on_April_25,_2020 "Cancelled UFC event on April 25, 2020").[[69]](#cite_note-SmithTeixeira-69) However, due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") event, Johnson was rescheduled to face [Khama Worthy](/wiki/Khama_Worthy "Khama Worthy") at [UFC 249](/wiki/UFC_249 "UFC 249").[[70]](#cite_note-70) In turn, the bout was postponed to a future date.[[71]](#cite_note-71) However, the event was cancelled.[[72]](#cite_note-72) However, on April 9, [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White"), the president of UFC announced that this event was postponed to a future date.[[73]](#cite_note-Postpone-73) Instead, Johnson faced [Thiago Moisés](/wiki/Thiago_Mois%C3%A9s "Thiago Moisés") on May 13, 2020, at [UFC Fight Night: Smith vs. Teixeira](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night:_Smith_vs._Teixeira "UFC Fight Night: Smith vs. Teixeira").[[74]](#cite_note-74) After outstriking Moisés in the first round, Johnson lost the fight via submission, less than thirty seconds into the second round.[[75]](#cite_note-75)

Johnson faced [Clay Guida](/wiki/Clay_Guida "Clay Guida") on February 6, 2021, at [UFC Fight Night 184](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_184 "UFC Fight Night 184").[[76]](#cite_note-76) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[77]](#cite_note-77)

#### Return to victory

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Michael_Johnson_(fighter)&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Return to victory")]

Johnson was scheduled to face [Alan Patrick](/wiki/Alan_Patrick_(fighter) "Alan Patrick (fighter)") on May 7, 2022, at [UFC 274](/wiki/UFC_274 "UFC 274").[[78]](#cite_note-78) However, the bout was pushed back, one week to [UFC on ESPN: Błachowicz vs. Rakić](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN:_B%C5%82achowicz_vs._Raki%C4%87 "UFC on ESPN: Błachowicz vs. Rakić"), for unknown reasons.[[79]](#cite_note-79) Johnson won the fight via knockout in the second round, snapping his three year long losing streak and getting his first knockout victory since 2016 in the process.[[80]](#cite_note-80)[[81]](#cite_note-81)

Johnson faced [Jamie Mullarkey](/wiki/Jamie_Mullarkey "Jamie Mullarkey") on July 9, 2022, at [UFC on ESPN: dos Anjos vs. Fiziev](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN:_dos_Anjos_vs._Fiziev "UFC on ESPN: dos Anjos vs. Fiziev").[[82]](#cite_note-82) He lost the fight via controversial split decision.[[83]](#cite_note-83) 15 out of 19 media outlets scored the fight in favor of Johnson.[[84]](#cite_note-84) The bout earned Johnson his third *[Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* bonus award.[[85]](#cite_note-UFCESPN39-85)

Johnson faced [Marc Diakiese](/wiki/Marc_Diakiese "Marc Diakiese") on December 3, 2022, at [UFC on ESPN 42](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_42 "UFC on ESPN 42").[[86]](#cite_note-86) He won the bout via unanimous decision.[[87]](#cite_note-87)

Johnson faced [Carlos Diego Ferreira](/wiki/Carlos_Diego_Ferreira "Carlos Diego Ferreira") on May 20, 2023 at [UFC Fight Night 223](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_223 "UFC Fight Night 223").[[88]](#cite_note-88) He lost the fight via knockout in the second round.[[89]](#cite_note-89)

Johnson faced Darrius Flowers on February 10, 2024, at [UFC Fight Night 236](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_236 "UFC Fight Night 236").[[90]](#cite_note-90) He dominated most of the bout on the ground and won by unanimous decision.[[91]](#cite_note-91)

Johnson faced [Ottman Azaitar](/wiki/Ottman_Azaitar "Ottman Azaitar") on December 14, 2024, at [UFC on ESPN 63](/wiki/UFC_on_ESPN_63 "UFC on ESPN 63").[[92]](#cite_note-92) He won the fight by knockout in the second round.[[93]](#cite_note-93) This fight earned Johnson another *[Performance of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[94]](#cite_note-UFCESPN63-94)

Johnson faced [Daniel Zellhuber](/wiki/Daniel_Zellhuber "Daniel Zellhuber") on July 19, 2025 at [UFC 318](/wiki/UFC_318 "UFC 318").[[95]](#cite_note-95) He won the fight by unanimous decision.[[96]](#cite_note-96)

Johnson was scheduled to face [Alexander Hernandez](/wiki/Alexander_Hernandez "Alexander Hernandez") on January 24, 2026 at [UFC 324](/wiki/UFC_324 "UFC 324").[[97]](#cite_note-97) However, the fight was cancelled on the day of the event, with the promotion giving no official reason. The cancellation followed notable betting‑line movement on the matchup, and it was reported that some observers speculated Hernandez may have been dealing with an injury, although this was not confirmed by the UFC.[[98]](#cite_note-98) UFC CEO and president [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White") later confirmed that the fight had been pulled from the card due to betting concerns.[[99]](#cite_note-99)

Johnson faced [Drew Dober](/wiki/Drew_Dober "Drew Dober") on March 7, 2026 at [UFC 326](/wiki/UFC_326 "UFC 326").[[100]](#cite_note-100) He lost the fight by knockout in the second round.[[101]](#cite_note-101)
